meta_title: For Freelancers: SaaS pricing to Solve can't finish projects (Intermediate Comparison)
meta_description: Learn how to SaaS pricing and overcome can't finish projects. A Intermediate-level Comparison using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Freelancers: SaaS pricing to Solve Project Stagnation
content.seo_description: Stop stalling on client builds. Learn how to implement strategic SaaS pricing models to ship faster using Antigravity and Claude Code for maximum efficiency.
content.answer_block: Project stagnation stems from scope creep and poorly defined monetization. To ship faster, implement a tiered SaaS pricing model early. By integrating Antigravity for rapid scaffolding and Claude Code for autonomous refactoring, you transform complex billing logic into a streamlined, automated engine that keeps your development momentum high.
content.intro:
The blinking cursor of an unfinished project is the graveyard of freelance ambition. You start with high-velocity enthusiasm, only to get bogged down in the 'feature swamp'—that treacherous middle ground where scope creep cannibalizes your profit margins and your client's patience. It isn't just a lack of discipline; it’s a failure of architectural scoping.

True velocity comes from treating your pricing model as the primary constraint of your development. By baking your monetization strategy into the codebase from day one, you force yourself to prioritize high-value features over vanity metrics. We are moving beyond manual implementation; we are utilizing Antigravity and Claude Code to automate the heavy lifting, ensuring you ship robust, revenue-ready SaaS products at a fraction of the traditional timeline.

steps.title: Define the Value-Based Pricing Tiers
steps.description: Establish your SaaS tiers before writing a single line of application code to ensure feature parity matches your revenue goals.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a clean, scalable Stripe-compatible subscription schema for a SaaS with three tiers: Hobby, Pro, and Enterprise, focusing on feature-gating logic.

steps.title: Automate Billing Logic Integration
steps.description: Use AI to scaffold the webhook handlers for subscription events, preventing manual errors in payment state management.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a robust Stripe webhook handler in TypeScript that updates user subscription status in Supabase based on invoice.payment_succeeded events.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --task 'implement stripe webhook handler with error logging'

steps.title: Implement Feature-Gating Middleware
steps.description: Create a centralized middleware to protect routes based on the pricing tier defined in Step 1.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Build a Next.js middleware that checks the user's subscription tier against requested API routes and redirects unauthorized users to the pricing page.

steps.title: Configure Automated Usage Limits
steps.description: Prevent resource abuse by programmatically enforcing usage limits per pricing tier.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a decorator or helper function that tracks API usage per user and throws a 402 Payment Required error if limits are exceeded.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --task 'add rate limiting based on subscription tier'

steps.title: Refactor for Production Velocity
steps.description: Use Claude Code to audit your codebase for performance bottlenecks, ensuring your pricing logic doesn't slow down the UI.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze the current codebase for performance optimization opportunities and suggest improvements for the subscription billing flow.

pro_tips: Always use a 'soft-gate' pattern for new features during development to allow beta testing without breaking current subscription logic.
Map your database schema directly to your pricing tiers to ensure that adding a new tier only requires a config change, not a database migration.
Implement an automated 'churn-saver' email trigger directly inside your webhook handler to capture feedback immediately when a user cancels.
Use mock billing data in your local environment via Stripe CLI to simulate edge cases like failed payments or trial expirations without moving real money.
When building for clients, provide a 'Pricing Configurator' UI so they can adjust prices without asking you to touch the code.
content.next_steps: Deploy your staging environment to Vercel, run a full integration test with the Stripe CLI, and then present the automated billing dashboard to your client for final sign-off.
content.conversion_block: Stop fighting the code and start building the future of your freelance business with tools that actually work for you. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
